data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — child 59ef's slice is FULLY covered; no per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to this child:
- Verified-fake FIDELITY half of parent SC1 ("Producer and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → 59ef AC1 (dual-run shape-contract harness over the enrichment surface get_comment_map/get_issuelinks_map/get_parent_map/search_issues, fake always + real live-gated) + AC2 (fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's). This pins the specific bug-0ee6 failure mode "tests pass against fictional shapes," complementing 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and fe57 (honest fixtures/fake foundation). FULLY.
- Contributes to AC1 intent (the shared snapshot shape stays honest at the producer/enrichment source) and to SC5/AC6 posture via 59ef AC3 (assertions check structure/type — keys + JSON primitive types — not exact values; anti-change-detector) and AC4 (live opt-in, no flake/slowdown of default suite).

DELEGATION — criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; declared OUT items (fixtures → story A/fe57; snapshot round-trip → story B/8937) are consistent with the roster:
- AC1 schema SSOT + AC2 round-trip contract / SC1 → 8937 (keystone) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures + fake foundation); 59ef adds the fidelity guarantee.
- AC3/SC2 (collapse dual outbound appliers) → 33d0.
- AC4/SC3 (sub-op telemetry; computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly) → 2359.
- AC5/SC4 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip; de-encode 'expected-bug' probe guards) → 822a.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: SC1 (verified-fake fidelity half) FULLY by THIS child; AC1 contributed (schema artifact → 8937); AC2/AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; SC5 posture clause applied per-test (incl. 59ef AC3); AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence, below).

ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; ALREADY tracked across siblings 2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57 AND on this ticket via two prior G3 audits): Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including 59ef (AC3), only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests"). Repo cross-check confirms docs/mutation-testing.md is a general mutation-testing doc, not the AC6 reconciler-test governing artifact, and no child references producing/extending it. Natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 (e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests"), or a small dedicated child.

This single AC6 gap is NOT re-filed as a new structured finding here — it is fully recorded on this ticket and across the sibling set; re-emitting it a seventh time would be duplicative. No NEW coverage finding for this pairing: child 59ef's owned criteria (verified-fake dual-run shape contract / SC1 fidelity half) are FULLY covered, and the only roster-level gap (AC6) is already tracked.
